name: Prone
description: A very low stance that changes collision height, movement pace, visibility, aiming profile, and the rules for standing back up.
design_notes: Prone is a stance with strong systemic consequences. It should define capsule dimensions, enter and exit windows, blocked stand-up behavior, camera offset, noise output, aiming limits, and interactions with stealth or cover. Because it can trap players under geometry, clearance checks and recovery options are more important than animation length.
design_purpose: Let players reduce their profile for stealth, cover, traversal under obstacles, or weapon stability.
Create a high-commitment stance with stronger concealment and lower mobility.
Support levels with crawlspaces, low cover, and deliberate infiltration pacing.
player_fantasy: The player feels hidden and deliberate, sacrificing mobility to become harder to see or hit.

parameters.name: prone_pace_units_per_second
parameters.description: Horizontal crawl velocity while prone is active.

parameters.name: prone_height_multiplier
parameters.description: Collision capsule height multiplier for prone stance.

parameters.name: prone_enter_window_ms
parameters.description: Committed transition interval when entering prone.

parameters.name: prone_exit_window_ms
parameters.description: Committed transition interval when leaving prone.

parameters.name: stand_clearance_margin_units
parameters.description: Extra headroom required before returning to crouch or stand.

parameters.name: prone_noise_multiplier
parameters.description: Noise reduction multiplier applied to movement sound events.

edge_cases: A player trying to stand under a low obstacle needs a blocked stand-up state rather than collision penetration.
Weapon aiming, camera pitch, and interaction prompts may need different offsets while prone_height_multiplier is active.
Moving platforms and slopes can invalidate prone clearance after the player entered safely.
AI visibility and projectile hitboxes must agree on whether prone changes target height.
common_bugs: Capsule height changes but camera and aim origin remain at standing height.
Stand-up clearance checks ignore dynamic blockers and pop the player into another character.
Prone movement uses standing friction and slides down gentle slopes unexpectedly.
Animation cancellation exits prone visually while collision_capsule_height remains low.
balancing_notes: Prone concealment should be strong only where level geometry and AI sightlines support it.
Very slow prone pacing works for infiltration but becomes tedious if crawlspaces are long or repeated.
Allowing full weapon accuracy while prone can make it a dominant combat stance unless countered by exposure or setup cost.
accessibility_notes: Provide toggle prone and clear blocked-stand messaging so players do not have to hold a button while crawling.
Avoid camera positions that create nausea or obscure exit visibility in tight spaces.

implementation_notes.unity: Store stance_state_id separately from animation_state_id. Change capsule height and center together, run Physics.CheckCapsule for stand_clearance_margin_units before exit, and update camera offset from stance state rather than animation pose.
implementation_notes.godot: Swap collision shape profiles through a stance controller and validate stand-up with shape queries before changing back. Move prone crawl in _physics_process so slopes and moving platforms use the same ground_contact_state as other locomotion.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a crouch-like custom stance when standard crouch is insufficient. Server validates prone entry and exit, while animation montages, camera offsets, and stealth tags read the replicated stance_state_id.
implementation_notes.web: Represent prone as a collision profile plus movement modifier in the fixed loop. Test stand clearance against dynamic bodies before applying exit, then update render skeleton and camera after simulation state commits.

example_games.title: Metal Gear Solid V: The Phantom Pain
example_games.note: Uses prone movement for stealth approach and concealment.

example_games.title: ARMA 3
example_games.note: Uses prone stance for low-profile movement and weapon handling.
